title: linaria multicaulis subsp. heterophylla
intro: Linaria multicaulis subsp. heterophylla, also known as Purple Toadflax, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, as well as skin conditions like acne and wounds. The health benefits of Purple Toadflax include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable addition to natural remedies. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for a range of herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
